sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain in the right flank radiating to the groin, a pain score of 4 out of 10. He reports a previous episode of hematuria and testicular pain in the past. The patient reports pain when urinating. He denies nausea, vomiting, and fever.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is mildly low. Heart rate is within normal limits. Neurologic examination is nonfocal.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a history of kidney stones and a kidney stone. Symptoms are consistent with a possible urinary tract infection.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order urinalysis and urine culture. Start ibuprofen 600mg every 6 hours as needed for pain. Schedule follow-up in 7 days to assess response to treatment.
